tables.title: Initial Franchise Fees

rows.description: Standard for all first-time franchisees except veterans, affiliate owners, school lunch, community development, or non-traditional
For qualified existing franchise owners in substantial compliance

rows.type: Reduced Fee for Affiliate Subway Owners/BDs
rows.description: For qualified Subway franchisees of affiliates

rows.type: Reduced Fee for US Armed Forces
rows.description: For honorably discharged US Veterans purchasing first franchise
For approved convenience store operators, food service cos, hospitals, universities, etc.
For adding individual owner not already a Subway franchisee
Waived if in same facility as Base Restaurant
If existing restaurants not in substantial compliance at opening

table_notes: Note 1: Financing available for low-density markets. Multi-unit operators may qualify for rebate if ahead of development schedule.
Note 2: If you don't qualify for reduced fee, must pay full $15,000.
Note 3: If representations not true at opening, full fee due.
Note 4: Oil company retailers with 50+ units converting existing sandwich shop: franchise fee waived.
Note 5: Satellite fee may be refunded if lease not obtainable within 90 days.
Note 6: Short-term satellite renewable for additional 1 year at $1,000.
Note 7: Satellite to full restaurant conversion may receive credit.

tables.title: Other Initial Fees

rows.description: If you need more than 12 months to open
Security deposit (2 months rent) plus $50 admin fee plus recording fees
Required purchase from franchisor or SFAFT
If you fail to attend or cancel with <10 business days notice
Required until Q4 2025; then Ingenico at $10/mo lease
SVS terminal for gift card processing only

table_notes: Extension fee non-refundable. Additional year with no further extensions.
Development program fee = current franchise fee × number of restaurants.

tables.title: Other Fees

rows.remarks: Note 1 — reduced rates 6.5%-8% for airports/stations; 7.5%-8% for DA holders
Note 2 — reduced 0.5%-2% for some non-traditional; 2%-3.5% for DA holders
Plus $50 bank change fee, $20 bounced check, collection costs
For extending franchise with <10 years remaining; max 10-year extension
Paid to affiliate or landlord; affiliate may profit from sublease spread
Gold Standard Insurance Program required; EFT payments

rows.amount: All liability, damages, costs, lawyers' fees

rows.remarks: Must stop using marks and return materials
Covers SubwayPOS and other tech; subject to annual change

rows.amount: 1.9% of gross sales per loyalty transaction
rows.remarks: 30.9% reimbursement on $2 Rewards and Surprise Rewards
Waived if remodel completed within 6 months (or 12 months for new)
Charged only for 'Fail' scores; $144.92 from Jan 2026

rows.amount: Half arbitration fee; full if withholding money
rows.remarks: Plus case management fees: $500 probation, $250 extension, $250 interim order
Sales tax on royalty, advertising, other amounts

table_notes: Note 1: Gross sales includes all sales. Royalty payable weekly due Friday. We may pay up to 1/3 of royalties to Business Developers.
Note 2: Advertising deposited into SFAFT. Legacy franchise agreements allow 2/3 vote to increase advertising; current agreements fix at 4.5%.
Note 3: Under-reporting by >2% triggers overdue amount plus all costs plus potential termination.
Note 14: Restaurant Technology Fee may be subject to annual change. Digital Technology Fee may be imposed in future.
Note 15: Required to accept Visa, MC, Discover, AmEx. Must use designated integrated payment solution.
Note 20: Restaurant Excellence Visits by third-party provider. No charge for periodic visits; Revisit Fee only for Fail scores.

tables.title: Estimated Initial Investment (Single Restaurant)
table_notes: Typical restaurant ~1,375 sq ft (range 300-2,000 sq ft). Monthly rent $1,000-$6,000.
Additional Funds: 3-month estimate only; does not include royalty, advertising, food costs, or owner's draw.
Fresh Forward 2.0 décor required for all new restaurants and relocations; remodel costs for existing locations may be higher.
Grease trap may add $10,000-$15,000 to leasehold improvements.
Impact fees may add $5,000-$25,000 depending on municipality.
Nevada requires licensed architect ($1,500-$3,000 additional).

tables.title: Table 3 — Status of Franchised Outlets (2022-2024)
table_notes: Column 4 (Opened) includes restaurants reopened after temporary closure. For 2024, reopens account for approximately 52% of outlets in this column.
Columns 5 and 8 include locations that may have been closed temporarily as of fiscal year end. Many will reopen in subsequent year.
Column 7 (Reacquired) represents franchises reacquired and immediately (usually within 1-2 days) resold to another franchisee. Also appears in Table 4 Columns 5 and 7.

tables.title: Table 4 — Status of Company-Owned Outlets (2022-2024)
table_notes: Numbers in Column 4 reflect restaurants reacquired and closed temporarily, then reopened in conjunction with resale.
Numbers in Column 6 include locations that may have been closed temporarily.
Franchisor does not currently intend to open company-operated restaurants but reserves the right.

tables.title: Table 5 — Projected Openings as of December 31, 2024

rows.default: Entire balance due + interest + costs + Sublease termination + FA default
All payments due + repossession + costs + FA default

table_notes: All equity holders must guarantee financing obligations.
General release required as condition of loans.
SBA Franchise Registry expedited processing available.
Franchisor may change or eliminate loan programs without notice.
